subject: F1000 Monthly Update - December 2014
body:
To ensure you receive F1000 emails, add info@updates.f1000.com to your safelist.

-----------------------------------------------------

Faculty of 1000 |  http://f1000.com                
Monthly Update  |  December 2014 

-----------------------------------------------------

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~    
Beat Ebola with better research sharing
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~  

F1000Research has launched an Ebola article collection [ http://f1000research.com/article-collections/ebola ] to enable all Ebola-related knowledge to be made available on a single platform within days of submission. All articles submitted to this collection will be prioritized and published free of charge. F1000Research has a dedicated phone line (+442071931030) to enable those working in affected areas to dictate their reports directly.

Peter Piot, the co-discoverer of virus, has also published an F1000Research [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-269/v1 ] appealing for everyone working on the current epidemic to publish all their findings and experiences rapidly and openly. In addition, all F1000Prime Ebola and Marburg virus Article Recommendations [ http://f1000research.com/article-collections/ebola ] are free to access.

Read more on our blog [ http://blog.f1000research.com/2014/11/12/beat-ebola-with-better-research-sharing-says-discoverer-of-virus/ ].

 
   F1000Prime                                
==================
  
Below is a selection of recommendations added by our Faculty of 10,000+ Members over the past month:

11 *

Viral hijack 
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718213486?key=sQ1y7whJfWOe0lb ]
This paper helps to clarify how RNA viruses can exploit cellular microRNAs to reduce innate immune responses in mammalian cells.
Trobaugh DW, Gardner CL, Sun C et al.
Nature. 2014; 506:245-8

9 *
Promoting pathogenicity 
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718321420?key=0nAU5wWVUPbsBzt ]
This paper presents a fascinating story about itaconic acid, which is essential for the survival of pathogens such as Yersinia pestis and Salmonella enterica in target host cells.
Sasikaran J, Ziemski M, Zadora PK et al.
Nat Chem Biol. 2014; 10:371-7

7 *
Ceritinib vs. crizotinib [ http://f1000.com/prime/718326531?key=bptSCEbC5GuzLiD ]
A ground-breaking phase I study shows that ceritinib is 20 times more potent than crizotinib against anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK)-rearranged non-small cell lung carcinoma.
Shaw AT, Kim DW, Mehra R et al.
N Engl J Med. 2014; 370:1189-97


F1000Prime Reports
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 

Open-access review journal [ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/ ], reporting on the hottest topics.

Management of lymphangioleiomyomatosis 
[ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/m/6/116 ]
Recent advances in understanding the causes of lymphangioleiomyomatosis (LAM) offer new hope for therapies targeting RhoA GTPases, which are currently undergoing phase 1 clinical testing.
F1000Prime Report by Taveira-DaSilva AM [ http://f1000.com/prime/thefaculty/member/499999771097533924 ], and Moss J [ http://f1000.com/prime/thefaculty/member/1969263258581474 ].
F1000Prime Rep 2014, 6:116

 ---------------
 See all recent F1000Prime Reports          
 >> http://f1000.com/prime/reports/                 
 ---------------   
    

---------------------- 

~~~~~~~~~
F1000 Specialists programme 
~~~~~~~~~

Join F1000's global network of science communication enthusiasts - become an F1000 Specialist [ https://www.linkedin.com/groups?home=&gid=8123808&trk=groups_most_recent-h-logo ].

----------------------

    
   F1000Research                      
======================

Our innovative open science publishing platform [ http://f1000research.com/ ] offering rapid publication, open peer review and open data.

Below is a selection of our latest peer reviewed articles:

RESEARCH ARTICLE
Late cardiac sodium current can be assessed using automated patch-clamp [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-245 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/4kj ]
Published: 16 Oct 2014
Chevalier m, Amuzescu B, Gawali V et al.
F1000Research 2014, 3:245

RESEARCH ARTICLE
CaMKII binding to GluN2B is important for massed spatial learning in the Morris water maze [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-193/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/3ud ]
Published: 12 Aug 2014
Stein IS, Donaldson MS, Hell JW.
F1000Research 2014, 3:193

RESEARCH ARTICLE
False memory susceptibility is correlated with categorisation ability in humans [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-154/v2 ] [v2; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/4k0 ]
Published: 16 Oct 2014
Hunt K, Chittka L.
F1000Research 2014, 3:15

OBSERVATION ARTICLE
Plasmodium falciparum infection rates for some Anopheles spp. from Guinea-Bissau, West Africa [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-243/v2 ] [v2; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/4n3 ]
Published: 05 Nov 2014
Sanford MR, Cornel AJ, Nieman CC et al.
F1000Research 2014, 3:243

 ---------------------------------------------
  Submit now to F1000Research          
  >> http://f1000research.com/author/submit        
 ---------------------------------------------- 

~~~~~~~~~ 
CiteAb Antibody Awards
~~~~~~~~~ 
 
We have partnered with CiteAb to deliver the very first CiteAb Antibody Awards! We recently launched our Antibody Validation Collection [ http://f1000research.com/article-collections/antibody-validation ], and these awards are great way to recognise companies and individuals working with and creating antibodies. Make an award nomination or find out more here! [ http://www.citeab.com/awards ]
 

     F1000Posters                 
======================

Open Repository [ http://f1000.com/posters ] for posters and slide presentations; completely free to view and submit. Below is a selection:


* IMMUNOLOGY *  

Autologous tumor lysate-loaded dendritic cell vaccination combined with sunitinib for metastatic renal cell carcinoma 
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1097306 ]
Kakimi K, Matshshita H, Enomoto Y et al.
F1000Posters 2014,
5:1798  

NEUROLOGICAL DISORDERS
Pseudophosphorylated tau leads to memory deficits ahead of structural abnormalities in an inducible new mouse model of tau induced-neurodegeneration 
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1097221 ]
Di J, Beharry C, Cohen L et al.
F1000Posters 2014,
5:1795

ECOLOGY
Evolutionary relationships of Atoposaurid crocodyliforms and evidence for allopatric speciation driving their high diversity in the late jurassic of Europe
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1097315 ]
Tennant J, Mannion J.
F1000Posters 2014,
5:1786


  ----------------------------------------------
  Deposit now        
  >> http://f1000.com/posters/fordepositors      
 ----------------------------------------------

 * Follow our blogs * 
  
F1000Prime Blog [ http://blog.f1000.com/ ]
F1000Research Blog [ http://blog.f1000research.com/ ]
 
 
----------------------


-----------------------------------------------------   

In addition to this Monthly Update, we also send out emails featuring deals and discounts for registered users. To sign up, registered users should visit MyF1000 via any F1000 product and opt-in to receive "Information from F1000 about special offers" (under Email Alerts).

You have received this message because you asked to receive the F1000 Monthly Update to your registered email address from Faculty of 1000. If you do not wish to receive any further such messages, you may unsubscribe [ http://www.f1000.com/unsubscribe/email?email=jeeproject@yahoo.com&emailAlertType=MONTHLY_UPDATES ] at any time. For further information or enquiries please contact Customer Services at info@f1000.com [ mailto:info@f1000.com ].

To ensure you receive F1000 emails, add info@updates.f1000.com to your safelist.
If this email is not displaying properly, please view in your usual browser.

Beat Ebola with better research sharing

F1000Research has launched an Ebola article collection to enable all Ebola-related knowledge to be made available on a single platform within days of submission.
